EGFR: Epidermal growth factor receptor — drives cell proliferation; mutations common in lung cancer
TP53: Tumor suppressor — regulates cell cycle and apoptosis; most frequently mutated gene in cancer
BRCA1: DNA repair gene — mutations increase breast/ovarian cancer risk
BRCA2: DNA repair gene — involved in homologous recombination; linked to breast cancer
KRAS: Oncogene — drives cell growth signaling; mutations common in pancreatic/lung/colon cancer
BRAF: Serine/threonine kinase — activates MAPK pathway; V600E mutation common in melanoma
PIK3CA: Phosphoinositide 3-kinase — regulates cell growth and survival signaling
PTEN: Tumor suppressor — negative regulator of PI3K/AKT pathway
MYC: Transcription factor — regulates cell growth and proliferation; amplified in many cancers
RB1: Retinoblastoma protein — tumor suppressor controlling cell cycle entry
APC: Tumor suppressor — regulates Wnt signaling; mutated in colorectal cancer
ABCB1: Drug efflux transporter — pumps drugs out of cells; key driver of multidrug resistance
ERBB2: HER2 receptor — overexpressed in ~20% of breast cancers; target of trastuzumab
ALK: Anaplastic lymphoma kinase — receptor tyrosine kinase; rearrangements in lung cancer
VEGFA: Vascular endothelial growth factor — promotes blood vessel growth in tumors
CDH1: E-cadherin — cell adhesion molecule; loss promotes invasion and metastasis
AKT1: Serine/threonine kinase — central node in PI3K/AKT survival pathway
MTOR: Mechanistic target of rapamycin — regulates cell growth, proliferation, and metabolism
MDM2: Negative regulator of TP53 — amplification leads to p53 inactivation
BCL2: Anti-apoptotic protein — prevents programmed cell death; target of venetoclax
CDKN2A: Tumor suppressor — encodes p16/p14ARF; frequently deleted in many cancers
FGFR1: Fibroblast growth factor receptor — amplified in lung squamous cell carcinoma
FGFR2: Fibroblast growth factor receptor — mutations in gastric and endometrial cancer
FGFR3: Fibroblast growth factor receptor — mutations in bladder cancer
MET: Hepatocyte growth factor receptor — amplification drives resistance to EGFR inhibitors
RET: Proto-oncogene — receptor tyrosine kinase; rearrangements in thyroid/lung cancer
NRAS: Oncogene — RAS family member; mutations in melanoma and leukemia
RAF1: Serine/threonine kinase — component of MAPK signaling cascade
MAP2K1: MEK1 kinase — downstream of RAF in MAPK pathway; target of trametinib
ERBB3: HER3 receptor — heterodimerizes with ERBB2; role in drug resistance
JAK2: Tyrosine kinase — mutations drive myeloproliferative disorders
STAT3: Transcription factor — activated by JAK signaling; promotes survival
SRC: Non-receptor tyrosine kinase — involved in cell proliferation and survival
ATM: DNA damage response kinase — senses double-strand breaks; mutations impair repair
ATR: DNA damage response kinase — responds to replication stress
CHEK1: Checkpoint kinase — mediates cell cycle arrest after DNA damage
CHEK2: Checkpoint kinase — tumor suppressor; germline mutations increase cancer risk
WNT1: Signaling ligand — activates Wnt/beta-catenin pathway in development and cancer
CTNNB1: Beta-catenin — transcriptional co-activator in Wnt pathway; mutated in liver cancer
NOTCH1: Transmembrane receptor — regulates cell fate decisions; mutations in T-ALL and breast cancer
SMO: Smoothened receptor — hedgehog pathway component; target of vismodegib
IDH1: Isocitrate dehydrogenase — mutations produce oncometabolite 2-HG; common in gliomas
IDH2: Isocitrate dehydrogenase — mutations in AML and gliomas
ARID1A: Chromatin remodeling — SWI/SNF complex member; frequently mutated in ovarian/gastric cancer
KDM6A: Histone demethylase — epigenetic regulator; mutated in bladder cancer
EZH2: Histone methyltransferase — silences tumor suppressors; target of tazemetostat
DNMT3A: DNA methyltransferase — epigenetic writer; frequently mutated in AML
TET2: DNA demethylase — epigenetic eraser; mutations in myeloid malignancies
NF1: Neurofibromin — RAS-GAP tumor suppressor; loss activates RAS signaling
VHL: Von Hippel-Lindau — tumor suppressor; loss drives clear cell renal carcinoma
